title: Push toggle reports success even when token API requests fail
reasoning: Both enable and disable flows ignore the HTTP response from /api/fcm-tokens. A 401, 403, validation error, or transient 5xx still flips the UI to enabled/disabled, leaving users with a state that does not match the server subscription record. For enable, this can make notifications appear enabled when no token was registered; for disable, it can make notifications appear disabled while the server may still retain a token.
reproduction: Make /api/fcm-tokens return a non-2xx response for register or unregister-all, click the toggle, and observe that the component still updates to the optimistic terminal state.
recommendation: Store the response, verify res.ok, and only update state after success. On failure, keep or reload the previous state and surface an error state/message rather than silently logging only unexpected exceptions.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests only cover TipTapRenderer behavior and TOC extraction; they do not mount PushNotificationToggle or mock failed /api/fcm-tokens responses.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that mocks requestPushToken plus /api/fcm-tokens returning 500 and asserts the switch does not become enabled; add the symmetric unregister failure case.
minimumFixScope: Update PushNotificationToggle.tsx handleEnable and handleDisable to check fetch responses and handle non-2xx outcomes.
